methods.description: This method involves measuring mammutid teeth using digital calipers and collecting additional measurements from published sources to analyze dental and postcranial elements.

steps.description: Mammutid teeth in the below noted repositories were measured using digital calipers for maximum crown length and width at each loph or lophid.
steps.searchable_summary: measure mammutid teeth using digital calipers

steps.description: Additional measurements were obtained from published sources, including Bravo-Cuevas, Morales-García & Cabral-Perdoma (2015), Gidley (1926), Green (2006), Green & Hulbert (2005), Harington (1977), Hay (1923Hay ( , 1926, Hibbard (1952), Hunt & Richards (1992), Lucas & Morgan (1997), Lundelius (1972), Mead, Haynes & Huckell (1979), Miller (1987), Osborn (1936), Pasenko (2011Pasenko ( , 2012, Richards (1984), Richards, Whitehead & Cochran (1987), Schwimmer (1991), Silverstein (2017), Trayler & Dundas (2009), and Woodman & Branstrator (2008).
steps.searchable_summary: collect additional measurements from published sources

steps.description: Measurements for specimens from Trolinger Spring and Boney Spring were calculated from graphs published in Saunders (1977) using GraphClick.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate measurements from graphs using GraphClick

methods.description: This method involves statistical analysis of the collected dental measurements, including calculating means, standard deviations, and conducting Shapiro-Wilk tests and T-tests.

steps.description: Means and standard deviations were calculated using Apple Numbers.
Shapiro-Wilk tests and T-tests were conducted using Wizard v. 1.9.18.

methods.description: This method describes the criteria for selecting and grouping specimens based on locality information and geological age for inclusion in the study.

steps.description: For inclusion in this study, all teeth needed to include at least state-or province-level locality information, and reasonable confidence as to geological age.
steps.searchable_summary: select teeth with locality information and geological age

steps.description: Where radiometric dates were not available, specimens were grouped by North American Land Mammal Age (NALMA) following Bell et al. (2004).
